sentences.business: There is a significant customer population here.
sentences.formal: There are various population segments to consider.
sentences.informal: There are so many people here!

words.usageNote: Day 9: 'There is a large population' / 'There are many populations'. Noun used with There is/are.

words.simpleMeaning: a chance to do something good

sentences.daily: There is an opportunity to grow here.
sentences.office: There are many opportunities in this company.
sentences.interview: There are exciting opportunities in this role.
sentences.business: There is a huge opportunity in this market.
sentences.formal: There are significant opportunities to explore.

words.usageNote: Day 9: 'There is an opportunity' / 'There are opportunities'. Singular vs plural with is/are.

words.simpleMeaning: a difficult task or situation that tests your ability

sentences.daily: There are many challenges in learning English.
sentences.office: There were several challenges last quarter.
sentences.interview: There are always challenges in a leadership role.
sentences.business: There is a challenge with supply chain management.
sentences.formal: There are notable challenges in the current environment.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a challenge / There are challenges. Both singular and plural forms used.

words.simpleMeaning: a way to solve a problem

sentences.daily: There is a solution to every problem.
sentences.office: There are multiple solutions available.
sentences.interview: There is always a solution if you look carefully.
sentences.business: There is a cost-effective solution for this.
sentences.formal: There are viable solutions being considered.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a solution / There are solutions. Very common in interviews and business.

words.simpleMeaning: something that can be used to help achieve a goal

sentences.daily: There are many resources available online.
sentences.office: There are insufficient resources for this project.
sentences.interview: There are many resources I used to prepare.
sentences.business: There is a resource gap in the team.
sentences.formal: There are adequate resources allocated.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a resource / There are resources. 'Resources' is often plural.

words.simpleMeaning: something that is needed or obligatory

sentences.daily: There is a requirement to study daily.
sentences.office: There are specific requirements for this project.
sentences.interview: There is a minimum experience requirement.
sentences.business: There are regulatory requirements to follow.
sentences.formal: There are strict requirements for compliance.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a requirement / There are requirements. Common in job descriptions.

words.simpleMeaning: an empty space or unoccupied position

sentences.daily: Is there a vacancy in this class?
sentences.office: There are three vacancies in our team.
sentences.interview: Is there a vacancy for a senior developer?
sentences.business: There are vacancies at all levels.
sentences.formal: There is a vacancy for the position of Manager.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a vacancy / There are vacancies. 'Job vacancy' = open position. Plural: vacancies.

words.simpleMeaning: a building or service provided for a particular purpose

sentences.daily: There is a gym facility here.
sentences.office: There are training facilities for employees.
sentences.interview: There are modern facilities in this company.
sentences.business: There is a state-of-the-art facility available.
sentences.formal: There are world-class facilities on campus.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a facility / There are facilities. 'Facilities' is always plural in general sense.

words.simpleMeaning: a section within an organization dealing with specific work

sentences.daily: There is a new department in our office.
sentences.office: There are five departments in the company.
sentences.interview: There was no HR department in my last company.
sentences.business: There are dedicated departments for each function.
sentences.formal: There are specialized departments handling compliance.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a department / There are departments. Common with There is/are/was/were.

words.simpleMeaning: a gathering of people for a discussion

sentences.daily: There is a meeting tomorrow.
sentences.office: There are three meetings scheduled today.
sentences.interview: There was a meeting about my promotion.
sentences.business: There will be a board meeting next week.
sentences.formal: There are weekly review meetings.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a meeting / There are meetings / There was a meeting / There will be a meeting — all forms!

words.simpleMeaning: a matter that is difficult to deal with

sentences.daily: There is a problem with my phone.
sentences.office: There are many problems in this workflow.
sentences.interview: There was a problem I solved in my last role.
sentences.business: There is a serious problem with delivery.
sentences.formal: There are systemic problems to be addressed.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a problem / There are problems / There was a problem. Most basic Day 9 sentence.

words.simpleMeaning: the circumstances existing at a particular time

sentences.daily: There is a difficult situation at home.
sentences.office: There was a challenging situation last week.
sentences.interview: There was a situation where I had to lead quickly.
sentences.business: There is a competitive situation in the market.
sentences.formal: There are several complex situations to navigate.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is/was a situation. Very common in STAR interview answers: 'There was a situation where...'

words.simpleMeaning: a link or relationship between things

sentences.daily: There is a strong connection between practice and progress.
sentences.office: There are good connections in this network.
sentences.interview: There is a clear connection between my skills and this role.
sentences.business: There is a strong business connection here.
sentences.formal: There are several connections between these trends.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a connection / There are connections. 'Make connections' = build relationships.

words.simpleMeaning: an advantage or good effect

sentences.daily: There are many benefits of learning English.
sentences.office: There are several employee benefits here.
sentences.interview: There are significant benefits to joining your company.
sentences.business: There are financial benefits to this strategy.
sentences.formal: There are measurable benefits of this approach.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There are benefits / There is a benefit. 'Benefit from' = gain from: You benefit from practice.

words.simpleMeaning: a choice available in a situation

sentences.daily: There are many options to study English.
sentences.office: There are two options for the project.
sentences.interview: There are multiple options for solving this.
sentences.business: There are various pricing options available.
sentences.formal: There are limited options given the constraints.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is an option / There are options. 'No option but to' = forced to do something.

words.simpleMeaning: a connected system of people or things

sentences.daily: There is a strong network of learners here.
sentences.office: There are many networks within this industry.
sentences.interview: There is a wide professional network I have built.
sentences.business: There is a distribution network across India.
sentences.formal: There are robust networks supporting this initiative.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a network / There are networks. Also: 'There is a networking event' — Day 9 usage.

words.simpleMeaning: a set of rules made by an organization

sentences.daily: There is a new policy for online classes.
sentences.office: There are strict policies in this company.
sentences.interview: There was no clear policy when I started.
sentences.business: There will be a new pricing policy next year.
sentences.formal: There are compliance policies to be followed.
sentences.informal: Is there a policy for this?

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is/are/was/were/will be a policy. All five forms of There used with 'policy'.

words.simpleMeaning: development towards a better state

sentences.daily: There is great progress in my English.
sentences.office: There has been significant progress this month.
sentences.interview: There is visible progress in my results.
sentences.business: There is tremendous growth progress.
sentences.formal: There are clear indicators of progress.

words.usageNote: Noun (progress) and verb (to progress). Day 9: There is progress. Day 6: I have made progress.

words.simpleMeaning: a piece of work to be done

sentences.daily: There are many tasks to complete today.
sentences.office: There were urgent tasks to handle.
sentences.interview: There are tasks I excel at, especially planning.
sentences.business: There are tasks assigned to each team member.
sentences.formal: There are several pending tasks to review.
sentences.informal: Are there any tasks for me?

words.usageNote: Day 9: There are tasks / There were tasks. 'Task someone with' = give them responsibility.

words.simpleMeaning: a person applying for a job or position

sentences.daily: There is a candidate waiting outside.
sentences.office: There are five candidates for this role.
sentences.interview: There are many candidates, but I am the right one.
sentences.business: There are multiple candidates in the market.
sentences.formal: There are shortlisted candidates to be interviewed.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a candidate / There are candidates. 'Job candidate' is very common in HR context.

words.simpleMeaning: something successfully accomplished

sentences.daily: There are many achievements I am proud of.
sentences.office: There are notable achievements in her career.
sentences.interview: There are three key achievements I want to highlight.
sentences.business: There are significant achievements this year.
sentences.formal: There are measurable achievements to note.
sentences.informal: There are so many achievements to celebrate!

words.usageNote: Day 9: There are achievements. 'Proud achievement' — used in interviews.

words.simpleMeaning: a contest or rivalry between people/businesses

sentences.daily: There is a lot of competition in this field.
sentences.office: There is growing competition in the market.
sentences.interview: There is strong competition for this role.
sentences.business: There are many competitors in this space.
sentences.formal: There is intense competition in the industry.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is competition (uncountable) / There are competitors. 'Competitive' is the adjective.

words.simpleMeaning: an arrangement made between two or more parties

sentences.daily: There is an agreement to study together.
sentences.office: There is a new agreement with the vendor.
sentences.interview: There was no formal agreement in my previous role.
sentences.business: There will be a signed agreement by Friday.
sentences.formal: There are several binding agreements in place.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is/was/will be an agreement. 'Sign an agreement' = make it official.

words.simpleMeaning: facts or information that prove something is true

sentences.daily: There is evidence of my improvement in my scores.
sentences.office: There is clear evidence of growth.
sentences.interview: There is strong evidence of my leadership skills.
sentences.business: There is evidence that this strategy works.
sentences.formal: There is compelling evidence for this approach.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is evidence (uncountable). 'Evidence of' or 'evidence that'. Always singular with 'is'.

words.simpleMeaning: to help or assist someone or something

sentences.daily: There is a lot of support in this community.
sentences.office: There is strong management support for the team.
sentences.interview: There is continuous support available here.
sentences.business: There are support systems for all employees.
sentences.formal: There are adequate support mechanisms in place.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is support / There are support systems. Also Day 6: I have support. Versatile word.

words.simpleMeaning: how well someone does a task or job

sentences.daily: There is room for improvement in my performance.
sentences.office: There has been excellent performance this quarter.
sentences.interview: There are clear examples of strong performance in my work.
sentences.business: There is consistent performance across all teams.
sentences.formal: There is documented evidence of high performance.

words.usageNote: Multiple day connections: Day 7 had performance, Day 8 will have performed, Day 9 there is performance.

words.simpleMeaning: a plan to achieve a long-term goal

sentences.daily: There is a clear strategy for my English learning.
sentences.office: There is a defined strategy for Q4.
sentences.interview: There is a strategic approach in all my work.
sentences.business: There are several strategies being evaluated.
sentences.formal: There are long-term strategies to be deployed.
sentences.informal: Is there a strategy for this?

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a strategy / There are strategies. 'Strategic' is the adjective.

words.simpleMeaning: a new idea or way of doing something

sentences.daily: There is constant innovation in technology.
sentences.office: There are new innovations being introduced.
sentences.interview: There is a culture of innovation in this company.
sentences.business: There are several innovations in the pipeline.
sentences.formal: There are significant innovations to be implemented.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is innovation (uncountable) / There are innovations (countable). Formal business word.

words.simpleMeaning: the state of being dedicated to a cause or activity

sentences.daily: There is a strong commitment to quality here.
sentences.office: There is a commitment to employee growth.
sentences.interview: There is a demonstrated commitment in my track record.
sentences.business: There are commitments made to the client.
sentences.formal: There are binding commitments in place.
sentences.informal: Is there commitment from the team?

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is commitment / There are commitments. 'Show commitment' = demonstrate dedication.

words.simpleMeaning: the ability to lead or guide others

sentences.daily: There is excellent leadership in this team.
sentences.office: There has been strong leadership through the crisis.
sentences.interview: There are clear examples of leadership in my history.
sentences.business: There is visionary leadership at the top.
sentences.formal: There are leadership qualities I bring to every role.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is leadership (quality). Day 4: The leader is strong. Multiple day usage.

words.simpleMeaning: a duty or obligation you are expected to fulfil

sentences.daily: There are many responsibilities in a leadership role.
sentences.office: There is a shared responsibility for results.
sentences.interview: There are responsibilities I managed independently.
sentences.business: There are responsibilities defined in the contract.
sentences.formal: There are critical responsibilities attached to this post.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There are responsibilities / There is a responsibility. 'Take responsibility' = own your mistakes.

words.simpleMeaning: information about performance to help improve it

sentences.daily: There is useful feedback in this test.
sentences.office: There is regular feedback from management.
sentences.interview: There is always room for feedback in my work style.
sentences.business: There are feedback mechanisms in place.
sentences.formal: There is constructive feedback available.
sentences.informal: Is there any feedback for me?

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is feedback. Uncountable noun — always 'feedback' never 'feedbacks'. 'Give feedback' = provide comments.

words.simpleMeaning: a need for or desire for something

sentences.daily: There is high demand for English speakers.
sentences.office: There is increasing demand for skilled workers.
sentences.interview: There is demand for the skills I offer.
sentences.business: There are unmet demands in the market.
sentences.formal: There is significant demand for these services.
sentences.informal: There's a huge demand for this!

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is demand. 'In demand' = wanted by many. 'Demand for' a product or skill.

words.simpleMeaning: the process of getting better

sentences.daily: There is visible improvement in my English.
sentences.office: There has been continuous improvement.
sentences.interview: There are clear improvements in my performance.
sentences.business: There are measurable improvements this quarter.
sentences.formal: There is evidence of significant improvement.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is improvement / There are improvements. Day 7: There had been improvement before the review.

words.simpleMeaning: the process of increasing or developing

sentences.daily: There is incredible growth in my vocabulary.
sentences.office: There has been strong revenue growth.
sentences.interview: There is consistent growth in my career record.
sentences.business: There is a growth strategy for next year.
sentences.formal: There is projected growth of 30%.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is growth. Uncountable. Day 8: By next year, we will have achieved 30% growth.

words.simpleMeaning: a branch of commercial or business activity

sentences.daily: There are many careers in every industry.
sentences.office: There are opportunities across industries.
sentences.interview: There is a lot I know about this industry.
sentences.business: There are emerging players in the industry.
sentences.formal: There are major shifts in the industry.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There are many industries / There is a growing industry. Connects with there is/are.

words.simpleMeaning: a person who uses the services of a professional

sentences.daily: There is a client waiting for me.
sentences.office: There are 10 clients on my list.
sentences.interview: There were clients I managed single-handedly.
sentences.business: There will be a new client joining next week.
sentences.formal: There are multiple clients to be handled.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a client / There are clients. All forms: is/are/was/were/will be + client.

words.simpleMeaning: a group of people working together

sentences.daily: There is a great team here.
sentences.office: There are five teams in the organization.
sentences.interview: There was a team of 15 I managed.
sentences.business: There will be a new team formed next quarter.
sentences.formal: There are dedicated teams for each service.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a team / There are teams. 'Team player' = someone who works well with others.

words.simpleMeaning: a planned piece of work with specific goals

sentences.daily: There is a new project starting today.
sentences.office: There are three projects running simultaneously.
sentences.interview: There were projects I led from start to finish.
sentences.business: There will be a project kickoff next Monday.
sentences.formal: There are critical projects under review.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a project / There are projects. Also Day 8: By May, we will have completed the project.

words.simpleMeaning: the money available for a purpose

sentences.daily: There is a limited budget for this.
sentences.office: There are strict budget constraints.
sentences.interview: There was a tight budget I worked within.
sentences.business: There will be a new budget review next year.
sentences.formal: There are allocated budgets for each department.
sentences.informal: Is there a budget for this?

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a budget / There are budgets. 'Budget' both noun and verb: budget for expenses.

words.simpleMeaning: a formal meeting to assess a candidate

sentences.daily: There is an interview scheduled for tomorrow.
sentences.office: There are interviews happening all week.
sentences.interview: There were challenging questions in the interview.
sentences.business: There will be a second-round interview next week.
sentences.formal: There are structured interviews being conducted.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is an interview / There are interviews. All tenses possible. Very relevant for learners.

words.simpleMeaning: trust in your own abilities

sentences.daily: There is growing confidence in my English.
sentences.office: There is confidence in the team's ability.
sentences.interview: There is confidence evident in my track record.
sentences.business: There is increasing confidence in the brand.
sentences.formal: There is considerable confidence in the outcome.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is confidence. Day 4: I am confident. Multiple day connection. Uncountable noun.

words.simpleMeaning: a regular pattern of behaviour

sentences.daily: There is a good habit of daily revision forming.
sentences.office: There are healthy work habits here.
sentences.interview: There are positive habits I have built over years.
sentences.business: There are habits that drive business success.
sentences.formal: There are established habits in this organization.
sentences.informal: There are some bad habits to break!

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a habit / There are habits. 'Build/develop a habit' = establish regular behaviour.

words.simpleMeaning: a regular sequence of activities

sentences.daily: There is a morning routine I follow every day.
sentences.office: There is a set routine in our office.
sentences.interview: There are strong routines that drive my productivity.
sentences.business: There are routines that ensure quality.
sentences.formal: There are standard routines that maintain consistency.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a routine / There are routines. 'Daily routine' is very common in English learning.

words.simpleMeaning: something you aim to achieve

sentences.daily: There are clear goals in my 75-day plan.
sentences.office: There are team goals for each quarter.
sentences.interview: There are ambitious goals I have set for myself.
sentences.business: There are strategic goals driving our vision.
sentences.formal: There are measurable goals defined for this project.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There are goals / There is a goal. Day 8: By year end, I will have achieved my goals.

words.simpleMeaning: a system of words used for communication

sentences.daily: There is a beautiful language I am learning.
sentences.office: There are several languages spoken here.
sentences.interview: There is one language I speak fluently — English.
sentences.business: There are multiple languages used in global business.
sentences.formal: There are numerous languages in India.
sentences.informal: How many languages do you know?

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a language / There are many languages. 'Mother tongue' = native language.

words.simpleMeaning: a formal request; or a program on a device

sentences.daily: There is an application form to fill out.
sentences.office: There are many applications submitted today.
sentences.interview: There are strong applications but mine stands out.
sentences.business: There are digital applications for every task.
sentences.formal: There are several applications pending review.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is an application / There are applications. Two meanings: job application + mobile app.

words.simpleMeaning: a formal test of knowledge

sentences.daily: There is an exam next week.
sentences.office: There were performance evaluations last month.
sentences.interview: There was an aptitude exam at the start.
sentences.business: There are compliance exams for all employees.
sentences.formal: There are several examinations to be conducted.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is an exam / There was an exam / There will be an exam. All tenses practiced.

words.simpleMeaning: an action that was wrong or incorrect

sentences.daily: There are mistakes to learn from.
sentences.office: There was a mistake in the report.
sentences.interview: There are no repeated mistakes in my record.
sentences.business: There are mistakes that can be avoided with planning.
sentences.formal: There were mistakes that were identified and corrected.
sentences.informal: There are too many mistakes here!

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a mistake / There are mistakes. Very common with There was/were in past context.

words.simpleMeaning: a response to a question or problem

sentences.daily: There is an answer to every question.
sentences.office: There are answers in the data.
sentences.interview: There is always an answer when you prepare well.
sentences.business: There are answers to customer concerns in the FAQ.
sentences.formal: There are clear answers provided in the report.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is an answer / There are answers. 'Answer' as verb: I answered the question correctly.

words.simpleMeaning: a specific instance that illustrates something

sentences.daily: There are many examples in this lesson.
sentences.office: There are examples of best practices here.
sentences.interview: There are strong examples of my leadership.
sentences.business: There are practical examples to follow.
sentences.formal: There are several examples cited in the report.
sentences.informal: Can you give me an example?

words.usageNote: Day 9: There are examples / There is an example. 'For example' = e.g. Introductory phrase.

words.simpleMeaning: a statement that tells you what to do or not to do

sentences.daily: There are many grammar rules to learn.
sentences.office: There are company rules to follow.
sentences.interview: There are professional rules I always follow.
sentences.business: There are legal rules that govern business.
sentences.formal: There are strict rules of conduct in place.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There are rules / There is a rule. 'Grammar rule' is very common in learning context.

words.simpleMeaning: a favourable circumstance for doing something

sentences.daily: There is always an opportunity if you look.
sentences.office: There are growth opportunities here.
sentences.interview: There are exciting opportunities in this role.
sentences.business: There is a huge market opportunity here.
sentences.formal: There are strategic opportunities to be explored.

words.usageNote: Repeated from id 177 to reinforce — critical Day 9 vocabulary. Singular: There is AN opportunity.

words.simpleMeaning: the arrangement or organization of parts

sentences.daily: There is a clear structure to every lesson.
sentences.office: There are well-defined structures in this company.
sentences.interview: There is a structure to how I approach problems.
sentences.business: There are structural changes planned for Q2.
sentences.formal: There are governance structures in place.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a structure. Day 1 grammar: SVO structure. Multiple day connection.

words.simpleMeaning: the set of words a person knows and uses

sentences.daily: There is new vocabulary to learn today.
sentences.office: There are specialized vocabulary sets for every field.
sentences.interview: There are vocabulary skills I have built over 10 days.
sentences.business: There is business vocabulary needed for every role.
sentences.formal: There are advanced vocabulary items in this lesson.
sentences.informal: There's so much vocabulary to learn!

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is vocabulary (uncountable) / There are vocabulary items (countable). Day 10 meta-word!

words.simpleMeaning: the process of reviewing material again to remember it

sentences.daily: There is a revision session every Day 10.
sentences.office: There is a revision of processes annually.
sentences.interview: There are revision strategies I have used to prepare.
sentences.business: There will be a policy revision next month.
sentences.formal: There are scheduled revisions built into the curriculum.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is revision / There are revisions. Day 10 is a Revision day — meta connection!

words.simpleMeaning: the capacity to develop into something in the future

sentences.daily: There is great potential in every student.
sentences.office: There is untapped potential in this team.
sentences.interview: There is significant potential I bring to this role.
sentences.business: There is market potential of $50 million.
sentences.formal: There are areas of significant potential.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is potential (uncountable). 'Full potential' = maximum capability. Use in interviews.

words.simpleMeaning: a feeling or opinion about something; the way you act

sentences.daily: There is a positive attitude in this group.
sentences.office: There are good attitudes in the team.
sentences.interview: There is a can-do attitude evident in my work.
sentences.business: There is a growth attitude in this company.
sentences.formal: There is a professional attitude maintained at all times.
sentences.informal: There's such a great attitude here!

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a positive attitude. Day 4: She is positive. Multiple day usage.

words.simpleMeaning: a formal public declaration

sentences.daily: There is an important announcement today.
sentences.office: There are several announcements pending.
sentences.interview: There was an announcement of my promotion.
sentences.business: There will be a product announcement next month.
sentences.formal: There are official announcements to be made.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is an announcement. All tenses: is/are/was/were/will be. Very natural with There.

words.simpleMeaning: a feeling of worry or anxiety about something

sentences.daily: There is a concern about my progress.
sentences.office: There are several concerns raised by the team.
sentences.interview: There are no concerns about my commitment.
sentences.business: There are valid concerns from customers.
sentences.formal: There are significant concerns to be addressed.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a concern / There are concerns. 'Raise a concern' = express a worry formally.

words.simpleMeaning: a position in a scale or hierarchy

sentences.daily: There is a new level of English to reach.
sentences.office: There are different levels in this company.
sentences.interview: There are multiple levels in this organization.
sentences.business: There are entry, mid, and senior levels.
sentences.formal: There are four levels of proficiency defined.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There are levels / There is a level. 'Level up' = improve your skills. CEFR levels: A1, A2, B1, B2, C1, C2.

words.simpleMeaning: the ability to do something well

sentences.daily: There are skills I have developed in 10 days.
sentences.office: There are valuable skills in this team.
sentences.interview: There are core skills I bring to this role.
sentences.business: There are soft and hard skills required here.
sentences.formal: There are key skills identified for this position.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There are skills / There is a skill. 'Soft skills' vs 'hard skills' — very common career distinction.

words.simpleMeaning: the worth or importance of something

sentences.daily: There is great value in this lesson.
sentences.office: There are strong company values here.
sentences.interview: There are values I bring that align with yours.
sentences.business: There is significant brand value.
sentences.formal: There are core values that guide decisions.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is value (uncountable) / There are values (plural). 'Value for money' = getting good worth.

words.simpleMeaning: the ideas, customs, and behaviour of a group

sentences.daily: There is a culture of learning here.
sentences.office: There is a positive work culture in this company.
sentences.interview: There is a culture of collaboration I bring.
sentences.business: There is a customer-first culture.
sentences.formal: There are cultural values that define the organization.
sentences.informal: Is there a good culture here?

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a culture / There are cultures. 'Company culture' = values and behaviour in an organization.

words.simpleMeaning: something that motivates you to do great things

sentences.daily: There is inspiration in every story of success.
sentences.office: There is daily inspiration from the leadership team.
sentences.interview: There is real inspiration in why I chose this field.
sentences.business: There are many sources of business inspiration.
sentences.formal: There are inspirational examples in this curriculum.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is inspiration (uncountable). 'Inspire' is the verb: This story inspires me.

words.simpleMeaning: formal meeting to evaluate someone for a position

sentences.daily: I am preparing for the interview.
sentences.office: She interviews candidates every Monday.
sentences.interview: My interview is scheduled for Friday.
sentences.business: We interview all shortlisted candidates.
sentences.formal: He was interviewed by three senior managers.

words.usageNote: Used across all days. Day 9: There is an interview. Day 7: I had an interview. Day 8: I will have completed an interview.

words.simpleMeaning: the act of formally showing or explaining something

sentences.daily: There is a presentation today.
sentences.office: There are weekly presentations by team leads.
sentences.interview: There was a presentation round in the final interview.
sentences.business: There will be a client presentation on Friday.
sentences.formal: There are structured presentations at the conference.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a presentation. Day 7: I had prepared a presentation. Day 8: By Friday, I will have given the presentation.

words.simpleMeaning: the feeling of pleasure from achieving something

sentences.daily: There is great satisfaction in learning.
sentences.office: There is high customer satisfaction.
sentences.interview: There is satisfaction in exceeding my targets.
sentences.business: There are satisfaction surveys conducted monthly.
sentences.formal: There is measurable satisfaction in the results.
sentences.informal: There's real satisfaction in this work!

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is satisfaction. 'Customer satisfaction' is a core business metric. Uncountable noun.

words.simpleMeaning: belief in yourself and your abilities

sentences.daily: There is rising confidence in my English.
sentences.office: There is confidence in the company's direction.
sentences.interview: There is visible confidence in my presentation style.
sentences.business: There is growing consumer confidence.
sentences.formal: There is sustained confidence in the market.
sentences.informal: There's a lot of confidence here!

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is confidence. Day 4: I am confident. Both forms useful for different contexts.

words.simpleMeaning: a connection between people or things

sentences.daily: There is a strong relationship between effort and results.
sentences.office: There are strong relationships with clients.
sentences.interview: There are positive relationships I have built in every job.
sentences.business: There is a long-term relationship with our supplier.
sentences.formal: There are professional relationships to be maintained.
sentences.informal: Is there a good relationship here?

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is/are relationship(s). 'Build/maintain a relationship' — key professional skill.

words.simpleMeaning: the time by which something must be done

sentences.daily: There is a deadline for submitting my homework.
sentences.office: There are tight deadlines this week.
sentences.interview: There was a deadline I met despite obstacles.
sentences.business: There will be a submission deadline on Monday.
sentences.formal: There are strict deadlines outlined in the contract.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a deadline. Day 8: By the deadline, I will have submitted. Cross-day connection.

words.simpleMeaning: a choice made after thinking about something

sentences.daily: There is an important decision to make.
sentences.office: There are critical decisions every day.
sentences.interview: There was a difficult decision I made and it worked.
sentences.business: There are strategic decisions to consider.
sentences.formal: There are formal decisions to be ratified.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is a decision / There are decisions. 'Make a decision' = decide. Never 'take a decision' in American English.

words.simpleMeaning: facts and statistics used for analysis

sentences.daily: There is useful data in this test.
sentences.office: There are large datasets to analyze.
sentences.interview: There is data that shows my consistent performance.
sentences.business: There are data-driven insights we use.
sentences.formal: There are sufficient data points for this analysis.
sentences.formal2: There is supporting data in the appendix.
sentences.informal: Is there any data on this?

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is data (uncountable, formal). In technical contexts: There are data points. Traditionally plural in academic writing.

words.simpleMeaning: the outcome or effect of an action

sentences.daily: There are excellent results from my revision.
sentences.office: There are quarterly results to be announced.
sentences.interview: There are strong results that prove my contribution.
sentences.business: There are measurable results of this campaign.
sentences.formal: There are positive results in every metric.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There are results / There is a result. 'Result in' = cause: This resulted in growth. 'Result from' = be caused by.

words.simpleMeaning: a strong effect on someone or something

sentences.daily: There is a big impact from daily practice.
sentences.office: There is a measurable impact on team performance.
sentences.interview: There is a clear impact of my contributions.
sentences.business: There are business impacts of every decision.
sentences.formal: There are significant impacts to be measured.

words.usageNote: Day 9: There is an impact / There are impacts. 'Make an impact' = create a significant effect. High-value interview word.
